requirement: As a user, I can create an organization.
As a user, I can edit an existing organization.
As a user, I can delete an organization that has no assigned employees.
notes: Deletion is blocked when employees are still assigned.

requirement: As a user, I can view organization headcount in the organization list.
As a user, I can create an employee.
notes: Employee requires name, organization, capacity, and type constraints.

requirement: As a user, I can edit an existing employee.
notes: Includes name, role, type, organization, manager, location, and capacity.

requirement: As a user, I can delete an employee.
notes: Related assignments are deleted and direct reports are unassigned.

requirement: As a user, I can assign an employee to an organization.
notes: Employee must belong to an organization.

requirement: As a user, I can classify an employee as an IC or a leader.
notes: Allowed values are IC and L.

requirement: As a user, I can assign a manager only from employees marked as leaders.
notes: Manager dropdown and backend validation must agree.

requirement: As a user, I can leave a leader without a manager.
notes: Supports top-of-hierarchy leaders such as CEOs.

requirement: As a user, I must assign a manager when an employee is an IC.
notes: ICs cannot be saved without a manager.

requirement: As a user, I cannot assign an employee to manage themselves.
As a user, I cannot create a circular manager hierarchy.
As a user, I cannot change a leader with direct reports into an IC until their reports are reassigned or removed.
As a user, I can view employee type, organization, manager, location, and capacity in the employee table.
notes: List view should reflect saved data.

requirement: As a user, I can filter the employee table by organization.
As a user, I can create a project.
As a user, I can edit an existing project.
As a user, I can delete a project.
As a user, I cannot save a project whose end date is before its start date.
As a user, I can create an assignment between an employee and a project.
notes: Requires employee, project, dates, and allocation.

requirement: As a user, I can edit an existing assignment.
As a user, I can delete an assignment.
notes: Assignment should be removed from all views.

requirement: As a user, I cannot save an assignment whose end date is before its start date.
As a user, I cannot save an assignment with allocation less than or equal to 0 or greater than 100%.
As a user, I can view assignments in a table with employee, project, dates, allocation, and notes.
As a user, I can export assignments to CSV.
notes: CSV includes employee, project, dates, and allocation.

requirement: As a user, I can view an employee schedule timeline.
notes: Accessible from the quick timelines panel.

requirement: As a user, I can view a project staffing timeline.
notes: Accessible from the quick timelines panel.

requirement: As a user, I can view an assignment relationship graph connecting employees to projects.
notes: Graph appears on the main matrix page.

requirement: As a user, I can view allocation watchdog charts over a configurable date range.
As a user, I can identify overallocated employees when utilization exceeds 100%.
As a user, I can open a canvas view of projects and assignments.
As a user, I can view employees grouped under their manager in the canvas resource list.
notes: Hierarchy is visible in the sidebar.

requirement: As a user, I can expand or collapse a manager to show or hide direct reports in the canvas resource list.
As a user, I can drag an employee from the canvas resource list onto a project to start creating an assignment.
As a user, I can filter canvas resources by organization.
As a user, I can edit employee details from the canvas.
As a user, I can edit project details from the canvas.
As a user, I can remove assignments from the canvas.
notes: Removal is available through canvas actions.

requirement: As a user, I can inspect project staffing details on the canvas.
notes: Project detail view shows weekly FTE.

requirement: As a user, I can rely on employee, project, assignment, and organization pages to stay in sync after changes.
notes: Create, update, and delete actions should refresh dependent views.

requirement: As a user, I can rely on employee hierarchy data to persist across page reloads.
notes: Manager relationships and employee type should round-trip through the API.
